title: Store Intelligence — Inventory & OOS
description: Inventory health, on-shelf availability, out-of-stock classification (chronic, true, phantom, at-risk), reorder points, demand signals, and supply-chain risk.

question: Which products have inventory risk?
Show me all out-of-stock products
On-shelf availability rate by store this week
Show me chronic out-of-stock problems
Which stores have the lowest on-shelf availability?

description: Inventory + product + category + warehouse + region + demand signals + forecasts with risk_flag. One row per product x warehouse.
OOS classification per store x product: TRUE_OOS, PHANTOM_OOS, AT_RISK_OOS, CHRONIC_OOS, or OK. Point-in-time snapshot.
OOS type: TRUE_OOS (off-shelf + zero sales 7d), PHANTOM_OOS (off-shelf + warehouse has stock + recent sales), AT_RISK_OOS (on-shelf + high velocity), CHRONIC_OOS (off-shelf >=50% of 14d), OK
Daily on-shelf availability scans by store x product x date. Use for trend analysis.

content: You are a Retail Inventory Analyst. Help users understand stock health, on-shelf availability, stockout risks, and replenishment needs.
Inventory data is at warehouse level (inventories has wh_id), not store level. OSA data (visual_osa_daily) is at store level.
Use vw_inventory_health for warehouse/product inventory questions. Use vw_osa_daily for store-level shelf availability.
Risk flags in vw_inventory_health: DISCONTINUED (is_disc=1), PERISHABLE_LEAD_TIME_RISK (perishable + lead time > shelf life), OK.
On-shelf availability rate = SUM(CAST(on_shelf_flag AS INT)) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) from vw_osa_daily.

OOS Classifications in vw_oos_classification: TRUE_OOS (off-shelf + zero sales 7d), PHANTOM_OOS (off-shelf + warehouse stock + recent sales), AT_RISK_OOS (on-shelf + >=2x avg velocity), CHRONIC_OOS (off-shelf >=50% of 14d, min 3 scans), OK.
When asked about stockout or OOS, always use vw_oos_classification and report the classification type. Cross-reference with vw_inventory_health for warehouse parameters.
Perishable products (is_perishable=1) deserve special attention — highlight shelf life vs lead time issues.
For trend questions, use vw_osa_daily grouped by osa_date.
When summarizing OOS, always report counts by classification type and highlight CHRONIC_OOS and TRUE_OOS as highest priority.

question: Which products have inventory risk?
sql:
SELECT prod_name, brand_name, cat_name, wh_name, reg_name,
  safety_stock_qty, reorder_point_qty, lead_time_days, risk_flag
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_inventory_health
WHERE risk_flag <> 'OK'
ORDER BY risk_flag, prod_name

question: Show perishable products where lead time exceeds shelf life
sql:
SELECT prod_name, cat_name, wh_name, reg_name,
  lead_time_days, shelf_life_days,
  lead_time_days - shelf_life_days AS days_over
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_inventory_health
WHERE is_perishable = 1 AND lead_time_days > shelf_life_days
ORDER BY days_over DESC

question: On-shelf availability rate by store this week
sql:
SELECT store_name, reg_name,
  COUNT(*) AS total_scans,
  SUM(CAST(on_shelf_flag AS INT)) AS on_shelf_count,
  SUM(CAST(on_shelf_flag AS INT)) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS osa_rate_pct
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_osa_daily
WHERE osa_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 7)
GROUP BY store_name, reg_name
ORDER BY osa_rate_pct ASC

question: Which products are most often off-shelf?
sql:
SELECT prod_name, cat_name,
  COUNT(*) AS total_scans,
  SUM(CASE WHEN on_shelf_flag = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS off_shelf_count,
  SUM(CASE WHEN on_shelf_flag = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS off_shelf_pct
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_osa_daily
WHERE osa_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 14)
GROUP BY prod_name, cat_name
ORDER BY off_shelf_pct DESC
LIMIT 10

question: On-shelf availability trend for dairy
sql:
SELECT osa_date,
  COUNT(*) AS scans,
  SUM(CAST(on_shelf_flag AS INT)) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS osa_rate_pct
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_osa_daily
WHERE depa_name = 'Dairy'
  AND osa_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 30)
GROUP BY osa_date
ORDER BY osa_date

question: Inventory overview for the Northwest warehouse
sql:
SELECT prod_name, cat_name, safety_stock_qty, reorder_point_qty,
  lead_time_days, demand_channel, forecast_horizon_days, risk_flag
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_inventory_health
WHERE wh_name LIKE '%Northwest%'
ORDER BY risk_flag DESC, prod_name

question: Which stores have the lowest on-shelf availability?
sql:
SELECT store_name, store_type, reg_name,
  SUM(CAST(on_shelf_flag AS INT)) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS osa_rate_pct
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_osa_daily
WHERE osa_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 7)
GROUP BY store_name, store_type, reg_name
ORDER BY osa_rate_pct ASC
LIMIT 5

question: Show me all out-of-stock products
sql:
SELECT oos_classification, COUNT(*) AS product_count
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_oos_classification
WHERE oos_classification <> 'OK'
GROUP BY oos_classification
ORDER BY product_count DESC

question: Which products are truly out of stock?
sql:
SELECT store_name, prod_name, cat_name, brand_name,
  last_scan_date, units_sold_7d, days_off_shelf_14d, oos_classification
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_oos_classification
WHERE oos_classification = 'TRUE_OOS'
ORDER BY store_name, prod_name

question: Show me phantom out-of-stocks
sql:
SELECT store_name, prod_name, cat_name, brand_name,
  last_scan_date, units_sold_7d, revenue_7d,
  has_warehouse_inventory, safety_stock_qty, oos_classification
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_oos_classification
WHERE oos_classification = 'PHANTOM_OOS'
ORDER BY revenue_7d DESC

question: Which products are at risk of going out of stock?
sql:
SELECT store_name, prod_name, cat_name,
  units_sold_7d, orders_7d, revenue_7d,
  safety_stock_qty, reorder_point_qty, lead_time_days, oos_classification
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_oos_classification
WHERE oos_classification = 'AT_RISK_OOS'
ORDER BY units_sold_7d DESC

question: Show me chronic out-of-stock problems
sql:
SELECT store_name, prod_name, cat_name, depa_name,
  days_scanned_14d, days_off_shelf_14d, off_shelf_pct_14d,
  units_sold_7d, oos_classification
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_oos_classification
WHERE oos_classification = 'CHRONIC_OOS'
ORDER BY off_shelf_pct_14d DESC

question: OOS summary by store — which stores have the most issues?
sql:
SELECT store_name, reg_name,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification = 'TRUE_OOS' THEN 1 END) AS true_oos,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification = 'PHANTOM_OOS' THEN 1 END) AS phantom_oos,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification = 'AT_RISK_OOS' THEN 1 END) AS at_risk,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification = 'CHRONIC_OOS' THEN 1 END) AS chronic_oos,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification <> 'OK' THEN 1 END) AS total_issues
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_oos_classification
GROUP BY store_name, reg_name
HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification <> 'OK' THEN 1 END) > 0
ORDER BY total_issues DESC

question: OOS breakdown by category — which departments are worst?
sql:
SELECT depa_name, cat_name,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification = 'TRUE_OOS' THEN 1 END) AS true_oos,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification = 'PHANTOM_OOS' THEN 1 END) AS phantom_oos,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification = 'AT_RISK_OOS' THEN 1 END) AS at_risk,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification = 'CHRONIC_OOS' THEN 1 END) AS chronic_oos,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification <> 'OK' THEN 1 END) AS total_issues
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_oos_classification
GROUP BY depa_name, cat_name
HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN oos_classification <> 'OK' THEN 1 END) > 0
ORDER BY total_issues DESC

question: Identify chronic out-of-stocks for perishable products
sql:
SELECT store_name, prod_name, cat_name,
  days_off_shelf_14d, off_shelf_pct_14d,
  lead_time_days, oos_classification
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_oos_classification
WHERE oos_classification = 'CHRONIC_OOS' AND is_perishable = 1
ORDER BY off_shelf_pct_14d DESC
